implementationAlgorithm: Trim the input title string to remove leading and trailing whitespace.
If the trimmed input is empty, block processing and return an empty input blocker error.
Convert all characters in the input to lowercase ASCII, removing or replacing any non-ASCII characters as needed.
Remove all unsupported punctuation characters from the input.
Replace all whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, etc.) with single hyphens ('-').
Collapse any sequences of multiple consecutive hyphens into a single hyphen.
Trim any leading or trailing hyphens from the resulting string.
If the resulting slug is empty after these transformations, block processing and return an empty input blocker error.
Otherwise, return a passed status with the generated slug key placeholder.

assumptions: The input title is a string that may contain whitespace, punctuation, and mixed case characters.
Unsupported punctuation characters are defined by the test intent and must be removed entirely.
Empty input after trimming or after all transformations is considered invalid and must be blocked.
forbiddenClaims: No durable product truth is claimed.
No additional criteria beyond those specified are added.
